title: Reply with exactly: reviewer OK
summary_text: **Current Path (as implemented)** 1. Scheduler runs reconciler before launching work: [kernel-scheduler.sh](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/scripts/kernel-scheduler.sh:357). 2. Reconciler first calls claim `rebuild` (TTL cleanup/state sync): [kernel-runtime-reconciler.sh](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/scripts/lib/kernel-runtime-reconciler.sh:254), [kernel-runtime-claim.sh](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/scripts/lib/kernel-runtime-claim.sh:524). 3. For each claim, reconciler does: - terminal queue item -> write stop file, kill pid, set `terminal` ([kernel-runtime-reconciler.sh](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/scripts/lib/kernel-runtime-reconciler.sh:298)). - ineligible queue item -> stop/kill, map reason to `awaiting_human` or `retry_queued` ([kernel-runtime-reconciler.sh](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/scripts/lib/kernel-runtime-reconciler.sh:312)). - live pid -> refresh `running`/`continuity_degraded` ([kernel-runtime-reconciler.sh](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/scripts/lib/kernel-runtime-reconciler.sh:325)). - stale by TTL -> release with `stale-claim-released` and archive artifacts ([kernel-runtime-reconciler.sh](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/scripts/lib/kernel-runtime-reconciler.sh:335)). 4. It also archives orphan compact files older `archive_ttl` with no active claim ([kernel-runtime-reconciler.sh](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/scripts/lib/kernel-runtime-reconciler.sh:346)). 5. `kernel-run-recovery.sh` is recovery/rehydration only (status + reattach/refresh compact), not cleanup logic: [kernel-run-recovery.sh](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/scripts/lib/kernel-run-recovery.sh:248), [kernel-run-recovery.sh](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/scripts/lib/kernel-run-recovery.sh:320). Validated tests: - [test-kernel-runtime-reconciler.sh](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/tests/test-kernel-runtime-reconciler.sh:33) (includes stale + orphan archive assertions). - [test-kernel-run-recovery.sh](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/tests/test-kernel-run-recovery.sh:85). - [test-kernel-runtime-run-driver.sh](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/tests/test-kernel-runtime-run-driver.sh:28). - [test-local-orchestration-heartbeat-cleanup.sh](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/tests/test-local-orchestration-heartbeat-cleanup.sh:7). **Proposed Cleanup Algorithm** 1. Classify each claim into `live`, `stop_requested`, `finalize`, `stale_release`, `orphan_artifact`. 2. `stop_requested` phase: - write stop marker + send TERM once. - do not immediately hard-finalize unless queue says terminal and pid is gone. 3. `finalize` phase: - if pid dead or envelope terminal, set final state and archive. 4. `stale_release` phase: - require `no live pid`, `no fresh envelope`, `age >= ttl`. - release claim and archive artifacts. 5. `orphan_artifact` phase: - compact/workspace receipt/envelope with no active claim and age >= `archive_ttl`. 6. Archive safety gate: - only move files under approved roots (state/workspace/compact). - if path outside roots, skip and report. 7. Idempotency: - write per-run archive manifest; re-runs should no-op if already archived. 8. Policy signal: - if runtime health indicates single-lane fallback, emit explicit `workflow_violation` in report (do not silently treat as normal degraded continuity). **JSON/Reporting Fields to Add** 1. `ttl_seconds`, `archive_ttl_seconds`, `duration_ms`, `dry_run`. 2. `stop_requested`, `finalized`, `stale_candidates`, `skipped_live`, `skipped_recent`. 3. `archived_by_category`: `compact`, `workspace_receipts`, `envelopes`, `workspaces`, `logs`, `traces`. 4. `archived_runs`: array of `{run_id, trigger, claim_identity, claim_age_seconds, files_archived, manifest_path}`. 5. `skipped_paths`: `{path, reason}` for unsafe/out-of-root items. 6. `workflow_violations`: array of `{run_id, code:"single-lane-fallback", evid...
